title: Port Scanning Metasploitable
description: Scan the victim machine and identify open doors.
problemStatement:
# Objective
Enumerate the open TCP ports on the Metasploitable VM and identify the services running.

## Steps
1.  Target the Metasploitable IP address you found in Task 1.
2.  Run a standard SYN scan (`-sS`) on the first 1000 common ports.
3.  Run a version detection scan (`-sV`) on the open ports found.
4.  Identify the following services if they exist:
    - FTP (Port 21)
    - SSH (Port 22)
    - Web Server (Port 80)
    - Samba/SMB (Port 139/445)

**Deliverable**: A screenshot or text log of the `nmap -sV` output showing the service versions (e.g., `vsftpd 2.3.4`).
hints: Default Nmap scans 1000 ports. That's fine for now.
Service version is crucial: It tells you which exploits might work!
expectedOutput: Port 21 open (vsftpd 2.3.4), Port 22 open (OpenSSH 4.7p1), Port 80 open (Apache httpd 2.2.8), Port 445 open (Samba smbd 3.X).
solutionCode:
# Replace IP with your Metasploitable IP
nmap -sV 192.168.56.101

# Optional: Scan all ports (takes longer)
# nmap -p- -sV 192.168.56.101
